title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to formal language theory– Alphabets, Strings, Concatenation
of strings, Languages. Regular Languages - Deterministic Finite State
Automata (DFA) (Proof of correctness of construction not required),
Nondeterministic Finite
State Automata (NFA), NFA with epsilon
transitions, Eliminating epsilon transitions, Equivalence of DFA and NFA,
Subset Construction.

title: Module 2
content:
The
formal
definition
of
a
regular
expression,
Building
Regular
Expressions, Equivalence with finite automata (Proof not expected) -
Converting FA to Regular Expressions, Converting Regular Expressions to
FA, Closure properties of Regular Languages (with proofs), DFA State
Minimization, Pumping Lemma for Regular Languages.

title: Module 3
content:
Formal definition of a context-free grammar, Designing context-free
grammars, Leftmost and Rightmost Derivations Using a Grammar, Parse
Trees, Ambiguous Grammars. Formal definition of a pushdown automaton,
DPDA and NPDA, Examples of pushdown automata – Normal Forms of
CFG – Chomsky and Greibach.

title: Module 4
content:
Linear bounded automaton, the formal definition of a Turing machine,
Examples of Turing machines - Turing machines as language acceptors,
Turing machines as computing functions, Variants of Turing Machines
(Proofs for equivalence with basic model not expected), Universal Turing
Machine,
Halting
Problem,
Recursive
and
recursively
enumerable
languages. Chomsky hierarchy of formal languages.
